data.title: India's Bharti Airtel posts higher quarterly profit on tariff hikes, one-time gain
data.description:
Indian telecom operator Bharti Airtel reported a higher quarterly profit on Thursday, as last year's tariff hikes continued to uplift its per-user revenue and helped by a one-time gain.

The company reported a consolidated net profit of 147.81 billion rupees ($1.69 billion) for the third quarter, from 24.42 billion rupees a year ago.

The company recorded a one-time gain of 75.46 billion rupees during the reporting quarter.

($1 = 87.5470 Indian rupees)

data.title: Market Chatter: AstraZeneca Investors Seek New Information Regarding China Chief's Detention
data.description:
Investors in AstraZeneca are seeking clarity regarding the arrest and investigation of the drugmaker's China chief Leon Wang, Reuters reported Wednesday.

AstraZeneca did not know the purpose of the investigation into Wang, also an AstraZeneca executive vice president, Reuters reported.

Investors could likely resume selling AstraZeneca stock even if its drug pipeline is among the strong ones unless the drugmaker releases new information about Wang's investigation, Reuters said, citing four healthcare investors and four analysts.

The dearth of information "blindsided" AstraZeneca, the report said, citing shareholder Lucy Coutts.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Bharti Airtel 3Q Rev INR455.990B Vs. INR383.393B
Teijin's Nine-Month Profit Balloons Nearly 377%
data.description:
Teijin recorded a 377% rise in attributable profit for the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, to 51 billion yen from 10.7 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Earnings per share were 264.43 yen in the nine months, up from 55.52 yen in the corresponding period of the last fiscal year.

The information technology company saw a 6.7% rise in revenue to 756.1 billion yen in the period from 708.8 billion yen a year prior.

The company expects an attributable profit of 25 billion yen, a basic EPS of 129.78 yen, a revenue of 1.01 trillion yen, and a total dividend of 50 yen per share for the fiscal year ending March 31.

Shares of the company were up nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Yum China Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Yum China Holdings, Inc., the largest restaurant company in China, has released its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company operates over 16,000 restaurants under six brands across more than 2,200 cities in China, including KFC, Pizza Hut, and Taco Bell.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Yum China reported a 4% year-over-year increase in total revenues, reaching $2.6 billion. Operating profit surged by 36% to $151 million, while diluted earnings per share (EPS) rose by 30% to $0.30. For the full year, total revenues hit a record $11.3 billion, a 3% increase from the previous year. Operating profit for the year grew by 5% to $1.2 billion, and diluted EPS increased by 18% to $2.33.

Business and Operational Highlights

Yum China opened 534 net new stores in the fourth quarter, bringing the total store count to 16,395 by year-end. Same-store sales reached 99% of the prior year's level, with same-store transactions growing 4% year-over-year. Delivery sales continued to grow, contributing approximately 39% of KFC and Pizza Hut's company sales.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

The company returned $1.5 billion to shareholders in 2024 through dividends and share repurchases. The Board declared a 50% increase in the cash dividend, raising it to $0.24 per share, payable on March 27, 2025. Yum China also plans to return a total of $3 billion to shareholders from 2025 through 2026.

Management's Perspective

Joey Wat, CEO of Yum China, highlighted the company's strong fourth-quarter performance, noting improvements in same-store sales, margins, and operating profit growth. Wat emphasized the effectiveness of the company's strategy in improving sales and profitability amid challenging market conditions. She also mentioned the success of initiatives like Project Fresh Eye and Project Red Eye, which have enhanced operational efficiency and competitiveness.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Yum China targets approximately 1,600 to 1,800 net new stores and capital expenditures in the range of $700 million to $800 million for the 2025 fiscal year. The company anticipates an increase in the franchise mix of net new stores for both KFC and Pizza Hut over the next few years.

SEC Filing: Yum China Holdings, Inc. [ YUMC ] - 8-K - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: Tradeweb Markets Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Tradeweb Markets Inc., a leading global operator of electronic marketplaces for rates, credit, equities, and money markets, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. The company reported significant growth across various financial metrics, reflecting its robust performance and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Tradeweb reported a 25.2% increase in quarterly revenues, reaching $463.3 million, compared to the same period last year. The company's net income for the quarter was $159.9 million, a 54.2% increase from the prior year period. Adjusted net income also saw a rise of 19.2%, amounting to $181.2 million. The diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter stood at $0.66, while the adjusted diluted EPS was $0.76.

For the full year 2024, Tradeweb achieved a 29.0% increase in total revenues, amounting to $1.7 billion. Net income for the year was $570.0 million, up 35.9% from 2023. The adjusted EBITDA margin for the year increased to 53.3%, and the diluted EPS rose to $2.33, a 36.3% increase from the previous year.

Business and Operational Highlights

Tradeweb's average daily volume (ADV) for the quarter was $2.3 trillion, a 36.7% increase from the prior year period. The company set quarterly ADV records in U.S. government bonds, mortgages, and global repurchase agreements. Additionally, Tradeweb achieved a record 7.9% share of fully electronic U.S. high yield TRACE.

In terms of revenue segments, the rates segment saw a 25.3% increase in revenues, reaching $240.2 million. The credit segment reported an 8.9% increase in revenues, amounting to $113.6 million. Equities revenues increased by 10.3% to $28.7 million, while money markets revenues surged by 166.5% to $44.3 million.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Tradeweb announced several strategic initiatives and corporate developments during the fourth quarter of 2024. The company appointed Enrico Bruni and Troy Dixon as Co-Heads of Global Markets, effective January 2025. Tradeweb also collaborated with the Tokyo Stock Exchange to enhance liquidity access for institutional investors in Japanese ETFs.

Additionally, Tradeweb celebrated the 10-year anniversary of its U.S. Credit platform and the five-year anniversary of portfolio trading in European Credit. The company became the first strategic partner for Goldman Sachs' GS DAP platform, integrating its trading and liquidity capabilities across fixed income.

Tradeweb's Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, a 20.0% increase from the prior year. The company also repurchased 478,915 shares of Class A common stock during the year, totaling $59.9 million.

Management's Perspective

Billy Hult, CEO of Tradeweb, commented on the company's performance: "2024 was a banner year for Tradeweb, marking our 25th consecutive year of record annual revenues. This achievement reflects our commitment to growing our geographical footprint, entering new markets and client channels through organic and inorganic collaborations, and consistently delivering value to our clients."

Hult highlighted the strong performance in the fourth quarter, driven by a favorable market environment and key partnerships, including the collaboration with Goldman Sachs and the Tokyo Stock Exchange. He expressed optimism for the future, emphasizing the opportunities ahead in 2025.

Future Outlook

Tradeweb provided guidance for the full year 2025, with adjusted expenses expected to range between $970 million and $1.03 billion. The company also projected acquisition and Refinitiv transaction-related depreciation and amortization expenses of $176 million. The assumed non-GAAP tax rate is estimated to be between 24.5% and 25.5%, and cash capital expenditures and capitalized software development are expected to be between $99 million and $109 million.

SEC Filing: Tradeweb Markets Inc. [ TW ] - 8-K - Feb. 06, 2025

(Nedre Vats, 6 February 2025): AutoStore Holdings Ltd (AutoStore: OSE:AUTO) invites investors and analysts to join the presentation of the fourth quarter 2024 financial results on Thursday 20 February 2025 at 08:00 CET via a live webcast and earnings call.

data.title: Gazprom Offloads Austrian, Italian Gas Trading Units
data.description:
Gazprom sold its Austrian and Italian gas trading units, Centrex Europe Energy & Gas and Centrex Italia, to Dubai-based investment company EGH Gulf in January, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing a regulatory filing.

The sale followed an arbitration ruling that ordered the Russian gas producer to pay Austrian state-owned petrochemical company OMV (OMV.VI) 233 million euros in damages over irregular German flows, which led to the halt of gas deliveries and the termination of the long-term supply deal between the parties.

EGH Gulf was set up in March 2024 with a focus on energy trading and production.

data.title: South Korea's FSC Proposes Rule Changes for Capital Markets Act
data.description:
South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) issued a preliminary notice of amendments to capital markets regulations, including the Financial Investment Services and Capital Markets Act, on Feb. 3, Monday, according to an FSC release the same day.

Key changes include legalizing fractional investment and securities lending intermediary platforms, enabling the trading of exchange-traded funds and exchange-traded notes through the alternative trading system, and tightening due diligence requirements for IPO bookrunners, the financial regulator said.

Other reforms cover stricter reviews for backdoor listings, expanding foreign currency bonds eligible for repos, and doubling the retail over-the-counter bond transaction limit to 10 billion won, it said.

The proposals are open for public feedback until March 17, Monday, with implementation expected by June 16, Monday.

data.title: Zuken's Nine-Month Profit Balloons Nearly 11%
data.description:
Zuken recorded an 11% rise in attributable profit for the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, to 2.63 billion yen from 2.36 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Basic EPS was 118.36 yen in the nine months, up from 104.07 yen in the corresponding period of the last fiscal year.

The software company saw a 5.5% rise in net sales to 28.7 billion yen in the period from 27.2 billion yen a year prior.

The company expects an attributable profit of 4 billion yen, a basic EPS of 181.39 yen, net sales of 41 billion yen, and a total dividend of 100 yen per share for the fiscal year ending March 31.

Shares of the company closed over 2% higher on Thursday.

data.title: Earnings Flash (XEL) Xcel Energy Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.81, vs. FactSet Est of $0.87
Brazil's finance minister sees inflation above target until June
data.description:
Brazil's finance minister expects inflation to "surprise positively" this year as the central bank tightens its monetary policy, but acknowledged in an interview aired late on Wednesday that the annual rate should remain above 4.5% until June.

Fernando Haddad told TV channel GloboNews that forecasts of a bumper crop this year and the recent gains the Brazilian real notched against the U.S. dollar, after hitting all-time lows in December, should help put inflation under control.

Private economists polled by the central bank on a weekly basis currently forecast inflation in Latin America's largest economy to end 2025 at 5.51%, exceeding the upper limit of the monetary authority's 1.5% to 4.5% target range.

"I think we might have positive surprises when compared to market expectations, just like we had for GDP," Haddad said, referring to the growth of Brazil's economy in 2024, which was much stronger than initially expected by market participants.

At the same time, he acknowledged that inflation would "probably" remain above 4.5% by June, as predicted by the central bank in the minutes of its latest monetary policy meeting.

Haddad attributed it to the lagged effects of tighter monetary conditions, but said that once prices start to slow down the response will likely be "much faster" than many in the financial market think.

Policymakers at Brazil's central bank raised their benchmark interest rate by 100 basis points to 13.25% last month and signaled a matching hike in March.

Haddad compared elevated borrowing costs to a medicine in the interview, saying that they should be applied but "at a dose that does not kill the patient."

President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has been a long time critic of what he sees as excessively high interest rates in the country.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Qualcomm, Arm, Skyworks, Ford, Honeywell, Strategy, FormFactor, Amazon, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures traded higher Thursday as investors looked past worries over tariffs and turned their attention to earnings, particularly from the tech sector.

These stocks were poised to make moves Thursday:

Qualcomm posted fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.75 a share, beating Wall Street estimates of $2.46, as revenue jumped 17% to $11.67 billion. Revenue at the company's QCT semiconductor business rose 20% in the period to $10.1 billion. But intellectual property licensing revenue of $1.54 billion missed estimates of $1.56 billion. Qualcomm said it expects fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.38 to $2.58 a share on revenue of $10.3 billion to $11.2 billion. Shares of the chip maker were falling 5.1% in premarket trading.

Chip designer Arm Holdings reported fiscal third-quarter adjusted earnings that beat analysts' estimates but its guidance for its fourth quarter matched forecasts, disappointing investors and sending the stock down 4.3%. For the fourth quarter, Arm said it expects adjusted earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.23 billion at the midpoint of its guidance. Wall Street anticipates fourth-quarter earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.22 billion.

Skyworks Solutions plunged 29% after the wireless chip maker said it expected its share of the business to supply Apple, its biggest customer, with radio frequency components would decline by 20% to 25%. The drop, Skyworks said in a conference call, would begin impacting revenue in its fiscal fourth quarter and throughout fiscal 2026. The company also named Philip Brace, who most recently served as CEO of 5G mobile company Inseego, as its next president and CEO.

Honeywell International announced Thursday a plan to break into three companies in an effort to unlock shareholder value. The industrial conglomerate also reported solid fourth-quarter earnings but issued profit and revenue guidance for 2025 that was below analysts' estimates. Shares rose 1.7%.

Ford Motor fell 5.9% after the auto maker posted fourth-quarter operating profit of $2.1 billion on sales of $48.2 billion, better than analysts' estimates on both metrics, but its forecast for operating profit this year of between $7 billion and $8.5 billion missed expectations of about $9.3 billion. Ford also said it expects breakeven adjusted earnings before interest and taxes in the first quarter because of "lower wholesales and unfavorable mix," and expenses related to the launch of some major U.S. assembly plants.

MicroStrategy, the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, rose 1.5% in premarket trading. The company, which announced Wednesday it now was doing business as Strategy, posted a fourth-quarter loss of $670.8 million, or $3.03 a share. Revenue fell 3% to $120.7 million and missed analysts' estimates of $122.4 million. The company also said it saw its largest-ever increase in quarterly Bitcoin holdings in the period, "culminating in the acquisition of 218,887 bitcoins acquired for $20.5 billion" since the third quarter. The company also said it has so far bought $20 billion of Bitcoin under a three-year plan of buying $42 billion in the cryptocurrency.

Molina Healthcare fell 8.3% after fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.05 a share missed analysts' expectations of $5.88 and the healthcare-services provider said it expected 2025 adjusted earnings of at least $24.50 a share, below analysts' estimates of $25.82.

FormFactor, the provider of test and measurement technologies, was falling 27% after issuing a weak first-quarter forecast. The company said it expects adjusted earnings of 15 cents to 23 cents a share on revenue of $165 million to $175 million. Analysts had been expecting adjusted earnings of 33 cents a share on revenue of $192.9 million.

Meta Platforms rose for a 13th-straight session on Wednesday, the stock's longest winning streak on record. Shares of the parent of Facebook and Instagram also set an intraday high of $718.14, and closed at a record high of $704.87. In premarket trading Thursday, Meta was up 0.2% at $707.26.

Earnings reports are expected Thursday from Amazon.com, Eli Lilly, Honeywell International, ConocoPhillips, Philip Morris International, Neurocrine Biosciences, Bristol Myers Squibb, Fortinet, Air Products, Cloudflare, Roblox, Hershey, and Take-Two Interactive Software.

Amazon.com was up 0.6% in premarket trading. Analysts expect the tech and online retailing giant to report fourth-quarter earnings of $1.49 a share on revenue of $187.3 billion. Wall Street will be watching Amazon's web services business closely after rival tech companies' cloud segments posted disappointing results. Amazon's report is scheduled for after the closing bell Thursday.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Irish Unemployment Rate Falls to 4%
data.description:
Ireland's seasonally adjusted unemployment rate fell to 4% in January 2025, down from a revised 4.5% in the previous month, as the number of unemployed persons decreased by 12.4 thousand to 116.2 thousand.

The jobless rate for men dropped to 4% (vs 4.9% in December), while that for women edged up to 4.1% (vs 4%).

Among individuals aged 25-74 years, the jobless rate declined to 2.9% from 3.3% in the prior month.

data.title: Qatar Trade Surplus Narrows Slightly
data.description:
The trade surplus in Qatar narrowed slightly to QAR 18.4 billion in December 2024 from QAR 18.7 billion in the corresponding month of the previous year.

Imports soared by 21.6% year-on-year to QAR 12.8 billion, while exports rose at a much softer pace of 6.7% to QAR 31.2 billion.

Considering the whole period of 2024, the country recorded a trade surplus of QAR 215.6 billion.

data.title: ING Groep's Q4 Earnings Fall, Revenue Flat
data.description:
ING Groep reported Q4 earnings Thursday of 0.37 euros ($0.38) per share, down from 0.45 euros a year earlier.

Three analysts polled by FactSet expected 0.39 euros.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31, expressed as total income, was 5.41 billion euros, unchanged from a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected 5.39 billion euros.

Shares of the company were down 3% in recent Thursday premarket activity.

data.title: Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Expects to Record Up to 30% Lower Attributable Profit in 2024
data.description:
Wenzhou Kangning Hospital expects a 20% to 30% lower net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent in 2024 compared with 2023, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The company attributed the expected decrease to an increase in medical insurance settlement differences, employee expenses, and taxes.

data.title: Linde Reports Full-Year and Fourth-Quarter 2024 Results (Earnings Release Tables Attached)
data.description:
Full-Year Highlights

  • Sales $33.0 billion, flat YoY, underlying sales up 2%

  • Operating profit $8.6 billion; adjusted operating profit $9.7 billion, up 7%

  • Operating profit margin 26.2%; adjusted operating profit margin 29.5%, up 190 basis points versus prior year

  • EPS $13.62 up 8%; adjusted EPS $15.51, up 9% or 10% ex FX

  • Returned $7.1 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases

  • Total project backlog of $10.4 billion

Fourth-Quarter Highlights

  • Sales $8.3 billion, flat YoY, underlying sales up 2%

  • Operating profit $2.3 billion, adjusted operating profit $2.5 billion, up 9%

  • Operating profit margin 27.4%; adjusted operating profit margin 29.9%, YoY up 250 basis points

  • EPS $3.60, up 14%; adjusted EPS $3.97, up 11% or 13% ex FX

2025 Guidance

  • First-quarter 2025 adjusted EPS guidance $3.85 - $3.95, represents 7%-9% growth ex. FX

  • Full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance $16.15 - $16.55, represents 8%-11% growth ex. FX

WOKING, GB / ACCESS Newswire / February 6, 2025 / Linde plc today reported fourth-quarter 2024 net income of $1,725 million and diluted earnings per share of $3.60, up 12% and 14% respectively. Excluding Linde AG purchase accounting impacts and other charges, adjusted net income was $1,899 million, up 8% versus prior year. Adjusted earnings per share was $3.97, 11% above prior year or 13% when excluding currency translation impact.

Linde's sales for the fourth quarter were $8,282 million, flat versus prior year or 2% above when excluding negative currency impact. Compared to prior year, underlying sales increased 2% from price attainment and stable volumes.

Fourth-quarter operating profit was $2,270 million. Adjusted operating profit of $2,480 million was up 9% versus prior year led by higher price and continued productivity initiatives across all segments. Adjusted operating profit margin of 29.9% was 250 basis points above prior year.

Fourth-quarter operating cash flow of $2,809 million increased 3% versus prior year. After capital expenditures of $1,250 million, free cash flow was $1,559 million. During the quarter, the company returned $1,990 million to shareholders through dividends and stock repurchases, net of issuances.

For full-year 2024, sales were $33.0 billion, flat versus 2023. Compared to prior year, underlying sales increased 2% from 2% price attainment and stable volumes. Operating profit was $8.6 billion and adjusted operating profit was $9.7 billion, 7% above prior year. Adjusted operating profit margin was 29.5% of sales, 190 basis points higher than 2023. Diluted earnings per share was $13.62 and adjusted diluted earnings per share was $15.51, up 9% versus prior year or 10% when excluding currency translation impact.

In 2024, Linde generated strong operating cash flow of $9.4 billion. The company invested $4.5 billion in capital expenditures and returned $7.1 billion to shareholders in the form of dividends and share buybacks, net of issuances.

Commenting on the financial results and business outlook, Chief Executive Officer Sanjiv Lamba said, "In 2024, the Linde team once again delivered industry leading results, including a 25.9% ROC, 29.5% operating margin and an EPS growth of 10% excluding currency. The results were delivered despite the challenging environment, reflecting the resiliency of our model and the exceptional execution by our employees. We also made significant progress in our clean energy strategy, signing our largest ever product supply agreement with a high-quality customer, supporting the $10 billion project backlog which will contribute to earnings growth for years to come."

Lamba continued, "As anticipated, global macro conditions have continued to weaken, especially foreign currency translation. Regardless, I'm confident that with our disciplined capital allocation and focused management actions Linde will continue to deliver shareholder value."

For the full year 2025, the company expects adjusted diluted earnings per share to be in the range of $16.15 to $16.55, up 4% to 7% versus prior year or 8% to 11% when excluding estimated currency headwinds. Full-year capital expenditures are expected to range between $5 billion and $5.5 billion to support operating and growth requirements, including the contractual sale of gas backlog. For the first quarter 2025, adjusted earnings per share is expected to be in the range of $3.85 to $3.95, 3% to 5% above prior-year quarter or 7% to 9% when excluding 4% assumed unfavorable currency.

Fourth-Quarter 2024 Results by Segment

Americas sales of $3,609 million grew 1% versus prior-year quarter. Compared with fourth quarter 2023, underlying sales increased 3% driven by 2% higher pricing and 1% volumes, primarily in the manufacturing, electronics and food & beverage end markets. Operating profit of $1,150 million was 31.9% of sales, 190 basis points above prior year.

APAC (Asia Pacific) sales of $1,668 million were 2% above prior year. Compared to prior year, underlying sales grew 1% driven by project start-up volumes, primarily in the electronics end market. Operating profit of $500 million was 30.0% of sales, 240 basis points above prior year.

EMEA (Europe, Middle East & Africa) sales of $2,059 million were down 2% versus prior year. Compared with fourth-quarter 2023, underlying sales grew 1%, driven by 3% higher pricing partially offset by 2% lower volumes. Volumes were lower versus prior year, mainly in manufacturing and chemicals and energy end markets. Operating profit of $686 million was 33.3% of sales, 400 basis points above prior year.

Linde Engineering sales were $628 million, 5% below prior year, and operating profit was $106 million or 16.9% of sales. Order intake for the quarter was $880 million and third-party sale of equipment backlog was $3.3 billion.

Earnings Call

A teleconference on Linde's fourth-quarter 2024 results is being held today at 9:00 am EST.

Live conference call

US Toll-Free Dial-In Number: 1 888 770 7292

UK Toll-Free Dial-In Number: 0800 358 0970

Access code: 6877110

Live webcast (listen-only)

https://investors.linde.com/events-presentations

Materials to be used in the teleconference are also available on the website.

About Linde

Linde is a leading global industrial gases and engineering company with 2024 sales of $33 billion. We live our mission of making our world more productive every day by providing high-quality solutions, technologies and services which are making our customers more successful and helping to sustain, decarbonize and protect our planet.

The company serves a variety of end markets such as chemicals & energy, food & beverage, electronics, healthcare, manufacturing, metals and mining. Linde's industrial gases and technologies are used in countless applications including production of clean hydrogen and carbon capture systems critical to the energy transition, life-saving medical oxygen and high-purity & specialty gases for electronics. Linde also delivers state-of-the-art gas processing solutions to support customer expansion, efficiency improvements and emissions reductions.

For more information about the company and its products and services, please visit www.linde.com

Adjusted amounts, free cash flow and return on capital are non-GAAP measures. See the attachments for a summary of non-GAAP reconciliations and calculations for adjusted amounts.

Attachments: Summary Non-GAAP Reconciliations, Statements of Income, Balance Sheets, Statements of Cash Flows, Segment Information and Appendix: Non-GAAP Measures and Reconciliations.

*Note: We are providing adjusted earnings per share ("EPS") guidance for 2025. This is a non-GAAP financial measure that represents diluted earnings per share (a GAAP measure) but excludes the impact of certain items that we believe are not representative of our underlying business performance, such as cost reduction and other charges, the impact of potential divestitures or other potentially significant items. Given the uncertainty of timing and magnitude of such items, we cannot provide a reconciliation of the differences between the non-GAAP adjusted EPS guidance and the corresponding GAAP EPS measure without unreasonable effort.

Forward-looking Statements

This document contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These forward-looking statements are identified by terms and phrases such as: anticipate, believe, intend, estimate, expect, continue, should, could, may, plan, project, predict, will, potential, forecast, and similar expressions. They are based on management's reasonable expectations and assumptions as of the date the statements are made but involve risks and uncertainties. These risks and uncertainties include, without limitation: the performance of stock markets generally; developments in worldwide and national economies and other international events and circumstances, including trade conflicts and tariffs; changes in foreign currencies and in interest rates; the cost and availability of electric power, natural gas and other raw materials; the ability to achieve price increases to offset cost increases; catastrophic events including natural disasters, epidemics, pandemics such as COVID-19, and acts of war and terrorism; the ability to attract, hire, and retain qualified personnel; the impact of changes in financial accounting standards; the impact of changes in pension plan liabilities; the impact of tax, environmental, healthcare and other legislation and government regulation in jurisdictions in which the company operates; the cost and outcomes of investigations, litigation and regulatory proceedings; the impact of potential unusual or non-recurring items; continued timely development and market acceptance of new products and applications; the impact of competitive products and pricing; future financial and operating performance of major customers and industries served; the impact of information technology system failures, network disruptions and breaches in data security; and the effectiveness and speed of integrating new acquisitions into the business. These risks and uncertainties may cause future results or circumstances to differ materially from adjusted projections, estimates or other forward-looking statements.

Linde plc assumes no obligation to update or provide revisions to any forward-looking statement in response to changing circumstances. The above listed risks and uncertainties are further described in Item 1A. Risk Factors in Linde plc's Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023 filed with the SEC on February 28, 2024, which should be reviewed carefully. Please consider Linde plc's forward-looking statements in light of those risks.

data.title: First Industrial Realty Trust Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
First Industrial Realty Trust, Inc., a leading fully integrated owner, operator, and developer of logistics real estate, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company has demonstrated robust performance across various financial and operational metrics, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market positioning.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, First Industrial reported a diluted net income available to common stockholders per share (EPS) of $0.52, down from $0.67 in the same period last year. The full year 2024 EPS was $2.17, compared to $2.07 in 2023. The company's funds from operations (FFO) for the fourth quarter was $0.71 per share/unit on a diluted basis, up from $0.63 per share/unit a year ago. Full year 2024 FFO was $2.65 per share/unit, reflecting an 8.6% growth from $2.44 in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

First Industrial's in-service occupancy was 96.2% at the end of the fourth quarter, an improvement from 95.0% at the end of the third quarter and 95.5% at the end of the fourth quarter of 2023. The company signed 4.7 million square feet of development leases in 2024, the second highest annual total since 2012, with 1.4 million square feet signed in the fourth quarter alone. Cash rental rates increased by 50.8% for the full year, marking the second consecutive annual increase greater than 50%.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the fourth quarter, First Industrial commenced development of two projects totaling 679,000 square feet with an estimated investment of $96 million. The company also sold five buildings for $25 million in the fourth quarter, contributing to a total of $163 million in sales for the full year 2024. Additionally, the board of directors declared a common dividend of $0.445 per share/unit for the first quarter of 2025, representing a 20.3% increase from the prior rate.

Management's Perspective

Peter E. Baccile, president and chief executive officer of First Industrial, highlighted the company's strong portfolio operating metrics and development leasing efforts. He expressed gratitude to the regional teams for their performance and emphasized the broad-based nature of the development leasing across ten markets.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2025, First Industrial has initiated NAREIT FFO guidance at a range of $2.87 to $2.97 per share/unit, indicating approximately 10% growth at the midpoint. The company expects to deliver growth in FFO per share driven by completed and future development leasing and continuing strong operating performance. The average quarter-end in-service occupancy is projected to be between 95.0% and 96.0%, with SS NOI growth on a cash basis before termination fees expected to be between 6.0% and 7.0%.

SEC Filing: FIRST INDUSTRIAL REALTY TRUST INC [ FR ] - 8-K - Feb. 05, 2025
